# terrible_community_general_statusio_maintenance (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `api_id` (String) Your unique API ID from status.io.
- `api_key` (String) Your unique API Key from status.io.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `statuspage` (String) Your unique StatusPage ID from status.io.

### Optional

- `all_infrastructure_affected` (Boolean) If it affects all components and containers.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `automation` (Boolean) Automatically start and end the maintenance window.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `components` (String) The given name of your component (server name).
- `containers` (String) The given name of your container (data center).
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `desc` (String) Message describing the maintenance window.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `maintenance_id` (String) The maintenance ID number when deleting a maintenance window.
- `maintenance_notify_1_hr` (Boolean) Notify subscribers 1 hour before maintenance start time.
- `maintenance_notify_24_hr` (Boolean) Notify subscribers 24 hours before maintenance start time.
- `maintenance_notify_72_hr` (Boolean) Notify subscribers 72 hours before maintenance start time.
- `maintenance_notify_now` (Boolean) Notify subscribers now.
- `minutes` (Number) The duration of the maintenance window (starting from playbook runtime).
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `start_date` (String) Date maintenance is expected to start (Month/Day/Year) (UTC). End Date is worked out from `start_date` + `minutes`.
- `start_time` (String) Time maintenance is expected to start (Hour:Minutes) (UTC). End Time is worked out from `start_time` + `minutes`.
- `state` (String) Desired state of the package.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `title` (String) A descriptive title for the maintenance window.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `url` (String) Status.io API URL. A private apiary can be used instead.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
